sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.83 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: b) Let$E_n$be a free locally nilpotent$n$-Engel group on countably many generators, and let$(E_n)$be the set of prime divisors of the orders of elements of the periodic part of$E_n$. It is known that$2,\,3,\,5 (E_4)$. Is it true that$(E_n)= (E_{n+1})$for all sufficiently large~$n$? { Yu.\,V.\,Sosnovski }

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
